academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in a relevant scientific field, must meet the specific curricular requirements, and are ranked through a competitive selection procedure.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) is required and must be obtained by 31 July 2026.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Accepted backgrounds include Food Science, Animal Science, Pharmaceutical Science, Biology, Biotechnology, Molecular Biology, Forest Science, Agricultural Science, Medicine or related fields, with proven competencies in the agri-food or bio-medical-health disciplinary areas.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based entry-title requirements apply. The selection awards up to 30 points for GPA or average grade, and applicants must obtain at least 50/100 overall to be included in the ranking.

accepted_proofs: One of the English certificates accepted by the University of Padua, with the minimum score required for English-taught degree programmes.
A degree programme title delivered entirely in English, documented as required by the University.
Exemption for native English speakers from the citizenship list accepted by the University.
exemption_rules: Applicants can use a degree completed entirely in English if the documentation explicitly states the language of instruction.
Native English speakers holding one of the nationalities listed by the University are exempt from submitting a certificate.
language_requirements.deadline: The programme-specific calls require the English language requirement to be achieved by 31 July 2026.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The second EU/EU-equated selection is a further selection aimed at filling any remaining vacancies.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The non-EU abroad call is a dedicated selection for non-EU candidates residing abroad.

required_documents.notes: The EU call accepts a valid identity document or passport; the non-EU abroad call requires a valid passport.

required_documents.document_name: Academic transcript and bachelor's degree diploma
required_documents.notes: Upload the full transcript with exams, grades and grading scale, plus the bachelor's diploma if already obtained.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required when academic documents are issued in another language.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate of at least B2 level or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: If not already available at application stage, it must be obtained and submitted by 31 July 2026.
Applicants must use the official template published with the programme page.
A CV is required as part of the selection dossier.
A motivation letter is required as part of the selection dossier.

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation, apostille or CIMEA Statement of Verification for academic documents
required_documents.notes: If admitted, applicants must submit one of these verification routes by 31 October 2026.
Required for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy at enrolment stage.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants when applicable under the University enrolment rules.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Selection by dossier review and interview
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants in the limited-access selection.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed interview date is stated in the extracted call text; interviews may be carried out online during the selection procedure.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Ranking out of 100 points: GPA up to 30, congruence up to 25, English up to 5, activities up to 25, interview up to 40. Applicants need at least 50/100 to be included in the ranking.

source_quotes.quote: This is a Limited access programme.
Call for non-EU students: 7 January 2026 – 7 March 2026
English language: B2 level (CEFR) or equivalent
necessary to achieve a total score higher than or equal to 50/100

uncertainty_notes: This is a new joint master's programme for 2026/27. The programme-specific call states that first-year activation remains subject to accreditation and that any curriculum also requires a minimum number of enrolled students.

Official 2026 sources are not fully aligned on annual fees: the programme page currently shows 4,000 euros per year, the EU foreign-qualification call states annual fees up to 4,000 euros, and the non-EU abroad call states annual fees up to 6,000 euros.
